skill.name: typescript-pro
skill.description: Master TypeScript with advanced types, generics, and strict type safety. Handles complex type systems, decorators, and enterprise-grade patterns. Use PROACTIVELY for TypeScript architecture, type inference optimization, or advanced typing patterns.
skill.summary: Guide advanced TypeScript architecture, strict typing, generics, decorators, and compiler-safe production patterns.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported five weak-cryptography hits, but review found no cryptographic code or unsafe algorithm usage in SKILL.md. The hits are false positives in prose about TypeScript types, interfaces, and type design, with no scripts, network access, file access, environment access, external commands, or prompt injection evidence.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Match
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md line 3 as weak cryptography, but the line is frontmatter prose describing TypeScript type safety. No cryptographic algorithm or implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed line is descriptive TypeScript metadata, not code. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic API.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Match
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md line 14 as weak cryptography, but the line describes designing TypeScript architectures and shared types. No cryptographic behavior is described.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a benign use-case bullet about TypeScript architecture. No executable code or cryptographic operation appears there.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Match
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md line 22 as weak cryptography, but the line says the skill is not for UI or UX design. This is benign scope guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed line contains non-security scope guidance. No weak cryptographic primitive or suspicious instruction appears there.

low_findings.title: Duplicate False Positive Weak Cryptography Match
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported a second weak-cryptography hit on SKILL.md line 22. Review found the same benign UI and UX scope guidance, with no security-sensitive code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: This duplicate points to the same benign line as the prior finding. There is no additional evidence of a cryptographic issue.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Match
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md line 43 as weak cryptography, but the line recommends robust interfaces and abstract classes. This is normal TypeScript design guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line discusses interface and class design, not cryptography. No unsafe algorithm, data handling, or execution mechanism is present.

content.value_statement: Complex TypeScript projects often lose safety when types grow across teams and packages. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward strict, maintainable TypeScript patterns.
actual_capabilities: Designs shared TypeScript types, interfaces, generics, and utility types for critical code paths.
Recommends strict compiler options and incremental build settings for production projects.
Improves type inference while avoiding unclear annotations and fragile type assertions.
Supports decorators, metadata programming, modules, namespaces, and framework integration guidance.
Suggests Jest or Vitest type assertions and TSDoc documentation for typed APIs.
limitations: Does not execute TypeScript compilers, test runners, or package managers by itself.
Requires project context to choose appropriate strictness, runtime targets, and module settings.
Cannot guarantee compatibility with unreleased TypeScript features without current documentation.
Focuses on TypeScript design, not JavaScript-only code or visual UI design.

use_cases.title: Design Shared Domain Types
use_cases.description: Create stable interfaces, discriminated unions, and generics for service boundaries and package APIs.

use_cases.title: Harden Existing Projects
use_cases.description: Move a codebase toward stricter compiler settings while controlling migration risk and developer friction.

use_cases.title: Improve Library Ergonomics
use_cases.description: Refine utility types, declaration files, and inference behavior for reusable TypeScript libraries.

prompt_templates.title: Review Type Safety
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a TypeScript feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this TypeScript module for type safety. Identify unclear types, unsafe assertions, and simple improvements that keep the code readable.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Strict Migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a step-by-step plan to enable stricter TypeScript compiler settings for this project without blocking normal development.

prompt_templates.title: Design Generic APIs
prompt_templates.prompt: Design generic TypeScript types for this API. Optimize for clear inference, safe constraints, and useful errors for callers.

prompt_templates.title: Architect Typed Boundaries
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate these TypeScript service boundaries. Recommend interfaces, runtime contracts, declaration files, tests, and build settings for long-term maintainability.

output_examples.input: A team wants to tighten TypeScript settings in a large application.
output_examples.output: A phased migration plan covering strictness flags, risk areas, CI checks, developer guidance, and fallback rules for legacy modules.

output_examples.input: A library has confusing generic parameters and weak inference.
output_examples.output: A redesign proposal that names generic roles, narrows constraints, improves defaults, and documents expected caller behavior.

output_examples.input: A package needs better public type declarations.
output_examples.output: A declaration strategy covering exported interfaces, utility types, TSDoc notes, compatibility concerns, and type-focused tests.

best_practices: Provide runtime targets, TypeScript version, framework, and current compiler options before asking for architecture advice.
Ask for migration steps when changing strictness in an existing codebase.
Pair advanced type patterns with tests and TSDoc so future maintainers can understand them.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for JavaScript-only guidance when TypeScript is not part of the build.
Do not request clever type tricks when simple interfaces would be clearer.
Do not apply strict compiler settings without checking build performance and team migration capacity.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps design advanced TypeScript types, strict compiler settings, typed APIs, declarations, and maintainable project architecture.

faq.question: Can it migrate a project to strict TypeScript?
faq.answer: It can plan and guide the migration, but it does not run compilers or modify every file automatically.

faq.question: Does it support React and Node.js projects?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill explicitly includes integration guidance for React, Node.js, Express, modules, and namespaces.

faq.question: Is it useful for library authors?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers generic APIs, utility types, type declaration files, inference quality, and TSDoc documentation.

faq.question: Does it require expert TypeScript knowledge?
faq.answer: No. You can ask for simple reviews or detailed architecture guidance depending on your project needs.

faq.question: Does the skill include executable scripts?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed package contains only SKILL.md guidance and no scripts, network calls, or command execution.
